title: Incident Response Procedure - Q1 2026 Update (PagerDuty Escalation Path)
body:
INCIDENT RESPONSE PROCEDURE UPDATE – PAGERDUTY ESCALATION PATH INTEGRATION

Effective Date: March 1, 2026
Document ID: comp_ir_pagerduty_q1_2026_001

1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE

This compliance note documents the updated incident response procedure for TechCorp's software infrastructure, incorporating the newly implemented PagerDuty escalation path. The purpose is to establish a standardized, multi-tiered incident notification and response process that ensures rapid acknowledgment and resolution of service incidents while maintaining clear roles and responsibilities across the engineering and operations teams.

This procedure applies to all incidents triggered on production services and critical infrastructure components monitored through PagerDuty. All engineering personnel, on-call responders, and technical leadership are required to follow this procedure.

2. PAGERDUTY ESCALATION POLICY FRAMEWORK

TechCorp has implemented a three-tiered escalation policy structure to balance response urgency with team capacity and expertise requirements:

Tier 1 (Primary Responder): The designated on-call engineer from the service owner team is notified immediately upon incident trigger. The Tier 1 responder has 30 minutes to acknowledge the incident. If no acknowledgment is received within 30 minutes, the incident automatically escalates to Tier 2.

Tier 2 (Secondary Responder): Upon escalation from Tier 1, the backup on-call engineer or team lead is notified. This responder has 30 minutes to acknowledge. Tier 2 responders are typically engineers from the same team with recent production context, ensuring continuity of understanding and faster resolution. If Tier 2 does not acknowledge within 30 minutes, escalation proceeds to Tier 3.

Tier 3 (Management Escalation): Critical incidents that remain unacknowledged after Tier 2 timeout escalate to technical leadership, including the relevant engineering manager and tech lead. Tier 3 is responsible for coordinating broader team resources
